description: Create a two-column content slide on control environment enhancement priorities with explanatory text on the left and a horizontal bar chart on the right.

header.kicker: SECOND ANNUAL EY-EFI GLOBAL INSURANCE RISK MANAGEMENT SURVEY
header.headline: What are the organization's top priorities as related to enhancing the control environment?

items.text: Planned enhancements for the control environment focus on the internal framework (cited by 48% of respondents, with 59% of EMEIA and 37% of Americas), followed by 38% who say improving control design technical capabilities (e.g., controls automation) and improved control performance indicators (35%) are the priorities.
items.sub: The implication is that CROs recognize the many different ways they can enhance and further mature the controls environments.

items.text: Proactive self-identification and reporting of control issues is increasingly the norm, with 41% of respondents saying they do so for 51%-75% of such issues and another 33% saying they do it for more than 75%.
items.sub: The use of AI and other forms of electronic communications and surveillance may also prompt further gains in proactive risk management. CROs expect technology to be a difference-maker; one CRO said, "In the future, we will be able to embed tech in a way that will tell me with some degree of precision the operating effectiveness of controls."

right.title: Figure 19: What are the organization's top priorities as related to enhancing the control environment?

series.label: Improve the internal controls framework (including standards and requirements pertaining to risk and control identification, risk assessment, control design and implementation, control performance indicators, testing and monitoring, governance, and oversight)
Improve control design including technical capabilities such as controls automation
Improve control performance indicators via metrics and reporting
Improve control oversight and testing performance across second-line and third-line, including better coordination and coverage
Improve control documentation, including control descriptions or process, risk and control taxonomy documentation
Risk and control identification (including key control tagging and population rationalization)
